cards.helpful_note: Repair does not mean minimizing. It means creating safety and accountability.
cards.safety_note: If the behavior involves force, coercion, fear, a large age or power difference, repeated secrecy, or contact between children, increase supervision and seek guidance from an appropriate child-safety, medical, or mental health professional.

cards.category_subtitle: Meet the body where it is
cards.say_this: You are not beyond help. The behavior still crossed a line that has to be addressed.
cards.do_this: Name the crossed line and the next safety steps without attacking the teen's worth.
cards.helpful_note: Teens need accountability without humiliation.
cards.safety_note: If the behavior involves force, coercion, fear, a large age or power difference, repeated secrecy, or contact between children, increase supervision and seek guidance from an appropriate child-safety, medical, or mental health professional.

cards.category_subtitle: Meet the body where it is
cards.say_this: If body feelings, questions, or urges feel hard to manage, the plan is to get safe support before anyone is involved.
cards.do_this: Create a concrete support plan and supervision plan.
cards.helpful_note: A prevention plan is safer than relying on willpower in the moment.
cards.safety_note: If the behavior involves force, coercion, fear, a large age or power difference, repeated secrecy, or contact between children, increase supervision and seek guidance from an appropriate child-safety, medical, or mental health professional.

cards.category_subtitle: Meet the body where it is
cards.say_this: Hands on your own body. Ask first.
cards.do_this: Gently move the child's hands back and model a simple ask, such as 'hug?' or 'space?'
cards.helpful_note: Very young children need concrete body rules repeated calmly.

cards.category_subtitle: Meet the body where it is
cards.say_this: That body needs space. Your hands stay here.
cards.do_this: Guide hands to the child's own lap, toy, or safe object.
cards.helpful_note: Physical redirection can teach the boundary without shame.

cards.category_subtitle: Meet the body where it is
cards.say_this: Stop touch. Gentle hands or no hands.
cards.do_this: Use a simple choice: gentle touch with permission or hands to self.
cards.helpful_note: Short phrases work best when impulse control is low.

cards.category_subtitle: Meet the body where it is
cards.say_this: Stop. Bodies need space. I am moving hands away.
cards.do_this: Create physical space immediately and stay close to supervise.
cards.helpful_note: The boundary needs to be immediate and calm.
cards.safety_note: If touch is forceful, coercive, sexualized, repeated after clear limits, or targets a vulnerable person, increase supervision and seek appropriate support.

cards.category_subtitle: Meet the body where it is
cards.say_this: Hands off. People get to have space.
cards.do_this: Block grabbing, climbing, or rough touch. Redirect hands to an object.
cards.helpful_note: This teaches that other bodies are not tools for regulation.
cards.safety_note: If touch is forceful, coercive, sexualized, repeated after clear limits, or targets a vulnerable person, increase supervision and seek appropriate support.

cards.category_subtitle: Meet the body where it is
cards.say_this: I will help your body stop touching.
cards.do_this: Move the child back and reduce access if the touch continues.
cards.helpful_note: A young child may need the adult to create the boundary physically and kindly.
cards.safety_note: If touch is forceful, coercive, sexualized, repeated after clear limits, or targets a vulnerable person, increase supervision and seek appropriate support.

cards.category_subtitle: Meet the body where it is
cards.say_this: That body needed space. Next time, ask first.
cards.do_this: Practice asking and waiting for an answer with a toy, doll, or adult who is comfortable helping.
cards.helpful_note: Practice turns body-safety rules into something the child can do.

cards.category_subtitle: Meet the body where it is
cards.say_this: You are learning. Hands stay on your own body unless someone says yes.
cards.do_this: Repeat the body rule calmly after the moment ends.
cards.helpful_note: Clear repetition builds early consent habits without shame.

cards.category_subtitle: Meet the body where it is
cards.say_this: Next time, say 'hug?' or choose a high five with permission.
cards.do_this: Practice one permission-based greeting.
cards.helpful_note: Simple alternatives reduce grabbing or intrusive touch.

cards.category_subtitle: Meet the body where it is
cards.say_this: Pause. Ask before touching another body.
cards.do_this: Coach the child to ask, then wait for the answer without pushing.
cards.helpful_note: Body boundaries are learned through repeated practice.

cards.category_subtitle: Meet the body where it is
cards.say_this: Your body wants closeness. The other person gets a choice.
cards.do_this: Offer a replacement: wave, fist bump, high five with permission, or sit nearby.
cards.helpful_note: This honors the need for connection while teaching consent.

cards.category_subtitle: Meet the body where it is
cards.say_this: Hands to yourself unless you hear yes.
cards.do_this: Have the child put hands on their own body or hold an object while waiting.
cards.helpful_note: A concrete body action helps impulse control.

cards.category_subtitle: Meet the body where it is
cards.say_this: Stop. That body said no or moved away. Hands off now.
cards.do_this: Step between bodies if needed and create space.
cards.helpful_note: The other person's no needs immediate protection.
cards.safety_note: If touch is forceful, coercive, sexualized, repeated after clear limits, or targets a vulnerable person, increase supervision and seek appropriate support.

cards.category_subtitle: Meet the body where it is
cards.say_this: I will not let you grab, climb, or touch after someone says stop.
cards.do_this: Move the child back and reduce access until they can keep space.
cards.helpful_note: Respecting stop is a safety rule, not a preference.
cards.safety_note: If touch is forceful, coercive, sexualized, repeated after clear limits, or targets a vulnerable person, increase supervision and seek appropriate support.

cards.category_subtitle: Meet the body where it is
cards.say_this: Bodies need space. You can be near people without touching them.
cards.do_this: Set a clear physical boundary, such as sitting beside instead of on or keeping arm's length.
cards.helpful_note: Specific distance helps the child know what to do.
cards.safety_note: If touch is forceful, coercive, sexualized, repeated after clear limits, or targets a vulnerable person, increase supervision and seek appropriate support.

cards.category_subtitle: Meet the body where it is
cards.say_this: The other person needed space. How can we respect that next time?
cards.do_this: Practice asking, hearing no, and choosing another way to connect.
cards.helpful_note: Repair builds empathy without forcing shame.

cards.category_subtitle: Meet the body where it is
cards.say_this: Wanting closeness is okay. Touching without permission is not okay.
cards.do_this: Have the child repair with a simple action, such as giving space or checking if the person is okay.
cards.helpful_note: This separates the need from the behavior.

cards.category_subtitle: Meet the body where it is
cards.say_this: Next time, ask once and accept the answer.
cards.do_this: Practice the exact words and what to do if the answer is no.
cards.helpful_note: The second half of consent is accepting no calmly.

cards.category_subtitle: Meet the body where it is
cards.say_this: Pause and check first. Other people get to choose what happens to their bodies.
cards.do_this: Prompt the child to ask clearly and accept the answer.
cards.helpful_note: Older kids need direct language about consent and personal space.

cards.category_subtitle: Meet the body where it is
cards.say_this: It looks like you want connection. Choose a way that respects space.
cards.do_this: Offer alternatives: sit nearby, ask for a hug, high five, talk, or take a sensory break.
cards.helpful_note: Connection needs can be met without crossing body boundaries.

cards.category_subtitle: Meet the body where it is
cards.say_this: Hands to yourself until someone clearly says yes.
cards.do_this: Set the expectation before entering group or public spaces.
cards.helpful_note: Pre-correction helps prevent impulsive touching.

cards.category_subtitle: Meet the body where it is
cards.say_this: Stop. That is not respectful or safe touch. Move back now.
cards.do_this: Separate bodies and require space before continuing the activity.
cards.helpful_note: A direct stop is needed when boundaries are ignored.
cards.safety_note: If touch is forceful, coercive, sexualized, repeated after clear limits, or targets a vulnerable person, increase supervision and seek appropriate support.

cards.category_subtitle: Meet the body where it is
cards.say_this: No means stop the first time. I am creating space now.
cards.do_this: Move the child away from the person affected and pause the activity.
cards.helpful_note: The rule protects everyone's body autonomy.
cards.safety_note: If touch is forceful, coercive, sexualized, repeated after clear limits, or targets a vulnerable person, increase supervision and seek appropriate support.

cards.category_subtitle: Meet the body where it is
cards.say_this: Touching after someone says stop is not okay. You need space from people right now.
cards.do_this: Set a temporary supervision or distance boundary.
cards.helpful_note: A temporary boundary prevents repeated harm while the child regains control.
cards.safety_note: If touch is forceful, coercive, sexualized, repeated after clear limits, or targets a vulnerable person, increase supervision and seek appropriate support.

cards.category_subtitle: Meet the body where it is
cards.say_this: We need to repair the boundary that was crossed.
cards.do_this: Have the child name the rule, give space, and make a plan for how to handle closeness next time.
cards.helpful_note: Repair should focus on respect and prevention.

cards.category_subtitle: Meet the body where it is
cards.say_this: Your need for connection is real. The other person's body boundary is real too.
cards.do_this: Discuss both parts and choose a replacement action for next time.
cards.helpful_note: This lowers shame while keeping the boundary clear.

cards.category_subtitle: Meet the body where it is
cards.say_this: Next time, ask once, wait, and accept the answer.
cards.do_this: Practice the ask, the wait, and the response to no.
cards.helpful_note: Practicing the no-response is what makes the boundary usable.

cards.category_subtitle: Meet the body where it is
cards.say_this: Check consent before touch. Closeness only works when the other person has a real choice.
cards.do_this: Use direct language and avoid teasing. Ask the teen to step back and reset.
cards.helpful_note: Teens need clear consent language that does not sound childish.

cards.category_subtitle: Meet the body where it is
cards.say_this: You may want connection or attention. Touch still requires permission.
cards.do_this: Offer a respectful alternative: ask, sit nearby, use words, or take space.
cards.helpful_note: Naming the need without excusing the behavior helps the teen stay engaged.

cards.category_subtitle: Meet the body where it is
cards.say_this: Pause. Make sure the other person wants the contact before you move closer.
cards.do_this: Coach a brief reset before rejoining the interaction.
cards.helpful_note: A pause can prevent social harm or safety concerns.

cards.category_subtitle: Meet the body where it is
cards.say_this: Stop. Touch after no is not okay. Move back now.
cards.do_this: Separate people and set a clear supervised boundary if needed.
cards.helpful_note: Consent violations need an immediate, serious boundary.
cards.safety_note: If touch is forceful, coercive, sexualized, repeated after clear limits, or targets a vulnerable person, increase supervision and seek appropriate support.

cards.category_subtitle: Meet the body where it is
cards.say_this: The other person's body boundary matters. I am creating space now.
cards.do_this: End the interaction and move the teen away from the person affected.
cards.helpful_note: A clear stop protects everyone and avoids minimizing.
cards.safety_note: If touch is forceful, coercive, sexualized, repeated after clear limits, or targets a vulnerable person, increase supervision and seek appropriate support.

cards.category_subtitle: Meet the body where it is
cards.say_this: This crossed a body boundary. We are stopping contact and getting space.
cards.do_this: Do not debate intent in the moment. Focus on separation and safety.
cards.helpful_note: Impact matters even if the teen says they did not mean harm.
cards.safety_note: If touch is forceful, coercive, sexualized, repeated after clear limits, or targets a vulnerable person, increase supervision and seek appropriate support.

cards.category_subtitle: Meet the body where it is
cards.say_this: We need to take the body boundary seriously and make a plan so it does not happen again.
cards.do_this: Discuss the crossed boundary, the impact, and the future supervision or consent plan.
cards.helpful_note: Repair requires responsibility and prevention, not just an apology.

cards.category_subtitle: Meet the body where it is
cards.say_this: Wanting closeness does not make crossed boundaries okay.
cards.do_this: Help the teen identify the need and a respectful way to meet it next time.
cards.helpful_note: This protects both accountability and dignity.

cards.category_subtitle: Meet the body where it is
cards.say_this: Next time, ask clearly, wait for a clear yes, and stop immediately if the answer is no.
cards.do_this: Practice or write the consent rule in a non-shaming way.
cards.helpful_note: Simple consent language gives the teen a concrete standard.
